flowcharts.title: Localized Disease: cTis or cT1a or Superficial cT1b

nodes.label:
Localized Disease: cTis or cT1a or Superficial cT1b
Branching: Clinical T-stage, Medical fitness for surgery
cTis or cT1a (medically fit)

Endoscopic Resection (ER)
Preferred for staging and potential cure
ER (EMR or ESD) is essential for accurate staging of early-stage cancers and can be therapeutic. Curative features include small size (≤2 cm), superficial invasion (T1a or T1b ≤500 µm), well or moderately differentiated, complete excision with negative margins.

Surgery
Appropriate for ≥T1b cancer or actively bleeding cancer, or when postoperative therapy is preferred
Gastrectomy with lymphadenectomy.
cTis or cT1a (nonsurgical candidate)

Endoscopic Resection (ER)
Recommended
ER is recommended for nonsurgical candidates with cTis or cT1a tumors.
Superficial cT1b (medically fit, potentially resectable)

Surgery
Recommended
Surgery as primary therapy is appropriate for ≥T1b cancer.
Adjuvant: Postoperative management depends on final pathologic stage and margin status (see GAST-4).

flowcharts.title: Locoregional Disease (cM0, Any N): Medically Fit, Potentially Resectable

nodes.label:
Locoregional Disease (cM0, Any N): Medically Fit, Potentially Resectable
Branching: Clinical T-stage, Biomarker status (MSI-H/dMMR)
cT2 or higher, Any N (non-MSI-H/dMMR)

Surgery
Option
Surgery as primary therapy is appropriate.

Perioperative Systemic Therapy
Category 1 recommendation
Perioperative systemic therapy is a category 1 recommendation for localized gastric cancer. Preferred regimens include FLOT (category 1) or fluoropyrimidine and oxaliplatin.

Consider Neoadjuvant/Perioperative ICI
Useful in certain circumstances
Consider neoadjuvant or perioperative immune checkpoint inhibitor(s) (ICI) if tumor is MSI-H/dMMR. Multidisciplinary evaluation is recommended.
cT2 or higher, Any N (MSI-H/dMMR)

Surgery
Option
Surgery as primary therapy is appropriate.

Perioperative Systemic Therapy
Option
Perioperative systemic therapy is an option.

Consider Neoadjuvant/Perioperative ICI
Useful in certain circumstances
In patients with a MSI-H/dMMR tumor, perioperative immunotherapy should be considered in consultation with a multidisciplinary team. Gastrectomy with appropriate lymphadenectomy remains the standard approach outside of prospective organ-preservation clinical trials.
Adjuvant: Response assessment is performed after neoadjuvant/perioperative therapy. Management is then based on surgical outcomes (GAST-4/GAST-5).

Adjuvant: Response assessment includes FDG-PET/CT (5–8 weeks after therapy) as clinically indicated, EGD and biopsy. If radiologic and endoscopic complete response occurs, gastrectomy remains the standard outside of organ-preservation trials. Immunotherapy treatment should be given for at least 1 year (counting perioperative and adjuvant settings).

flowcharts.title: Locoregional Disease (cM0, Any N): Medically Fit, Surgically Unresectable

nodes.label:
Locoregional Disease (cM0, Any N): Medically Fit, Surgically Unresectable
Branching: Surgical resectability after staging
Surgically unresectable after laparoscopic staging
Chemoradi (incomplete input)
